definition: Programs that run on a computer
example: We need to update the software.

definition: The physical parts of a computer
example: The hardware needs to be replaced.

definition: A set of steps a computer follows to solve a problem
example: The search algorithm shows relevant results.

definition: An organized collection of data stored electronically
example: Customer information is kept in a database.

definition: Made by humans, not natural
example: Artificial intelligence is transforming many industries.

definition: A system of connected computers
example: The office network is very fast.

definition: A secret word used to access a system
example: Choose a strong password.

definition: To copy a file from the internet to your device
example: I downloaded the app yesterday.

definition: To send a file from your device to the internet
example: Please upload your photo.

definition: To look through websites on the internet
example: I browse social media in the evening.

definition: To watch or listen to content online without downloading
example: We stream movies on Netflix.

definition: A piece of equipment made for a purpose
example: This device can track your steps.

definition: Remote servers accessed via the internet for storage
example: I save my files to the cloud.

definition: The practice of protecting systems from digital attacks
example: Cybersecurity is a growing concern.

definition: A new idea or method
example: This technology is a major innovation.

definition: Using machines to do work without human help
example: Automation has changed manufacturing.

definition: Relating to computer technology
example: We live in a digital age.

definition: Existing in a computer, not in the physical world
example: We had a virtual meeting on Zoom.

definition: Able to work together without problems
example: This app is compatible with Android.

definition: No longer used because something newer exists
example: Floppy disks are now obsolete.
